page_intent: transactional (single-intent — no dual-intent logic on this page; breed pages carry the informational/comparison depth)
meta_title: Puppy for Sale Singapore | AVS Licensed | Curious Tails
meta_description: Puppies for sale in Singapore from an AVS licensed breeder. Every puppy includes a $500+ starter kit, free home delivery, and a free AVS-certified training lesson. WhatsApp us.
h1_full_title_variant: Puppies for Sale Singapore — Trusted AVS-Licensed Breeder with Free Training & Starter Kit

entity_definitions.definition: Curious Tails is an AVS-licensed pet shop in Balestier Road, Singapore, specialising in puppy sales — Cavapoo, Maltipoo, Mini Dachshund, Corgi, Shiba Inu, Bichonpoo, and Cockapoo — each priced $2,888 to $4,488 and paired with a comprehensive starter kit, free home delivery, and a free first training lesson.

An AVS-licensed pet shop is a Singapore business approved by the Animal & Veterinary Service (a unit of the National Parks Board) to keep and sell dogs, cats, or rabbits, subject to welfare inspections, mandatory owner-suitability screening (the Pet Purchase Declaration), and annual licence renewal.
entity_definitions.placement: Definition Box, section 3 (within first 30% of page) — this is the Featured Snippet target

serp_analysis.dominant_format: Product/breed listing pages with trust and testimonial blocks
serp_analysis.intent_confirmation: Dominant intent is transactional — searchers want to see available puppies and a way to contact the seller; a secondary trust-verification intent runs underneath given the smuggling/scam awareness in this niche.
serp_features: People Also Ask (breed and cost questions)

serp_feature_targeting.featured_snippet: Definition Box answering 'what is an AVS-licensed pet shop' in <=40 words, placed in section 3, directly after the breed showcase.
serp_feature_targeting.image_pack: Every breed-card and lifestyle image gets unique alt text naming the breed + Balestier/Singapore + one sensory/action detail.
serp_feature_targeting.knowledge_panel: LocalBusiness + Organization schema reinforcing Curious Tails as a named, licensed entity at a fixed address, with AggregateRating from the 39 reviews.

competitor_weaknesses: Wag a Tail: limited opening hours, no delivery/setup or training mentioned
Dawgs & Co: no pricing shown anywhere, no AVS licence mentioned
The Pup Life: puppy pricing unclear, page focused on subscription meals
Across the board: no shop publishes a starter-kit value breakdown
Across the board: none explain what happens AFTER you reserve a puppy (process is opaque)
serp_analysis.consensus_topic_depth_note: Each of these gets 80-120 words of genuine detail on this page, not a one-line badge — e.g. 'health checks' names what is actually checked, not just the word 'health-checked'.
information_gaps: No competitor publishes a starter-kit itemised value breakdown (30+ items, $500+)
No competitor explains the free-training-lesson process end to end
No competitor walks a buyer through what happens after reservation (verified across Wag a Tail, Dawgs & Co, The Pup Life, and the review-focused search)
No competitor pairs AVS licence verification with real, cited smuggling case data (ICA/CNA/Straits Times) — this is a verified true gap, not a depth gap
No competitor offers lifestyle-first breed matching (all list breeds by availability, not by daily-routine fit)
vagueness_gaps: "Ethically bred and raised" (Dawgs & Co) → no definition of what the practice actually involves
"Health checked, vaccinated and dewormed" (multiple competitors) → no vaccine names, no schedule, no vet partner named
"4000+ families since 2016" (Wag a Tail) → no verifiable proof mechanism shown (no linked reviews count)
unanswered_questions: What happens in the first 24 hours after the puppy arrives at my home?
How do I know the starter kit is actually worth $500 and not marked up?
If my puppy gets sick in the first week, what actually happens?
sourcing_transparency_gaps: No competitor names their vaccination protocol or a specific vaccination guideline they follow
regulatory_compliance_gaps: No competitor explains HDB dog-ownership rules on their homepage, despite this being a primary buyer concern for Singapore's largely-HDB population
decision_stage_gaps: No clear 'what happens when I message you' process shown anywhere
No shop shows a value/inclusions breakdown next to the price to justify what a buyer is paying for
cro_conversion_gaps: No mid-page CTA on any competitor homepage — single CTA at top or bottom only
No urgency or availability signal (buyers can't tell if a breed is in stock)
No secondary conversion path for browsers not ready to buy today
No visible AVS licence number on any competitor homepage — trust signal left to inference

lsi_keywords.instruction: Adoption/free/cheap-cluster terms (e.g. 'free puppy Singapore', 'puppies for adoption Singapore') are EXCLUDED per business decision — that audience has no purchase intent and is not the target market for a $2,888+ puppy.
all: puppy for sale near me Singapore
puppy for sale Singapore AVS licensed
how to check pet shop licence
total cost of owning a puppy Singapore
best puppy breed for first time owner Singapore
independent dog breed for working owners
which is better Cavapoo or Maltipoo
what happens after buying a puppy
first week with new puppy Singapore
AVS accredited certified dog trainer scheme
check if pet shop is licensed
how to spot unlicensed pet seller
puppy vet visit schedule first year
what's included with puppy purchase Singapore
companion dog for existing dog Singapore
puppy for family with kids Singapore
lsi_keywords.type_coverage_note: Types 1-18 represented above: core variations, qualifiers, product/process terms, subtopics, action/intent phrases (reserve, verify, check, WhatsApp, matching), named entities (Curious Tails, Nelson and Kim, AVS, ACDT, ICA, SPCA, Wag a Tail, Dawgs & Co, The Lovely Pets — all verified via search), local/geo terms (Balestier, HDB, Singapore 320002), audience descriptors (first-time owner, working couple, family with kids), long-tail 4+ word phrases, methodology terms (lifestyle-first matching, breed selector), quantifier+noun (30+ items, $500+ value, 39 reviews, 2+ years), credential permutations (AVS licensed / AVS certified / AVS accredited / ACDT), cost/value terms, trust/social-proof terms, supply-chain/origin terms (health check, vet checked, microchipped), protection/risk terms (smuggling, parvovirus, unlicensed seller), everyday-reality terms (first night home, settling in checklist).

lsi_keyword_weighting.instruction: Home page is single-intent (transactional), so the informational cluster here exists to support topical authority and internal-link bridges to the Outer-tier pages (HDB guide, smuggling awareness, budget guide) — it is not competing for a separate secondary keyword ranking on THIS URL.

Practitioner's Note (first person, Nelson/Kim): 'We'll tell you not to buy the breed you came in for if it doesn't fit your day — ask Andrew Mak, our Cavapoo customer, who almost left with a Maltipoo'
Mid-page CTA: "Take the 2-minute breed quiz" -> links to breed selector
3+ distinct scenarios covered: working couple, retiree/helper at home, active outdoor family
content_outline.first_party_prompt: Use the Andrew Mak case study verbatim from expertise-library.md Case Study 1 — the exact situation, decision, and outcome. This is the page's strongest P1 proof point.

content_outline.heading: What Happens After You Reserve a Puppy
content_outline.cro_element: objection handled: 'what happens next, is this transparent?' + MAXIMUM-gain differentiator (free boarding is not offered anywhere in the crawled competitor set)
key_points: Step 1: WhatsApp us to confirm breed availability
Step 2: Place a deposit to secure the puppy
Step 3: Curious Tails boards the puppy for FREE (lodging and food both covered) until the customer is ready to receive it — no pressure to take the puppy home before you're prepared
Step 4: Delivery is scheduled around the customer's own availability, typically mornings or evenings
Step 5 (delivery day): Playpen setup + starter-kit walkthrough + training lesson booked
Verified gap: no competitor in the crawled set offers free boarding before delivery, or explains this sequence at all
content_outline.first_party_prompt: Use business-context.md Differentiation point 7 verbatim: free boarding (lodging + food) after deposit, delivery scheduled around the customer's availability (morning/evening).

content_outline.heading: Bringing a Puppy Home to an HDB Flat
content_outline.cro_element: regulatory compliance — objection handled: 'am I even allowed to keep this breed?'
key_points: HDB allows 1 dog of an approved breed/cross per flat; private property up to 3
Corgi and Shiba Inu are NOT on the standard HDB-approved list — say this plainly, don't bury it
Cavapoo, Maltipoo, Bichonpoo, Cockapoo, Mini Dachshund are commonly approved as crosses/small breeds — verify per HDB's actual list before publishing
Link out to full HDB breed guide for the complete list
content_outline.external_link_cue: HDB/AVS dog ownership rules — cite the actual approved-breed source, not a paraphrase

content_outline.cro_element: sourcing transparency + trust, handled once (not fear-based)
key_points: Every puppy is health-checked before it goes home
Vaccination follows the Singapore Vaccination Guidelines for Dogs and Cats (AVS/SVA) — covers core protection including parvovirus and distemper
Mentioned once, factually, not repeated as a scare tactic
[NEEDS INPUT] name of vet partner/clinic if the business wants to state one specifically

schema_plan: LocalBusiness (PetStore) — name, address, phone, hours, geo from GOOGLE_MAPS_EMBED, priceRange

WebPage — about property referencing subject_entity (AVS-licensed pet shop) as a Thing with alternateName 'pet shop licence Singapore'
FAQPage — from the 8 promoted FAQ entries
ItemList — the 7 breed cards in the showcase section, each an Item linking to its breed page

info_gain_score.pct_p1_p2: 18 of 21 substantive sections are P1/P2 = 86% (excludes the 2 P4-TERMINAL structural sections — Jump Links, visual-break image — from the denominator)
gain_drivers: Andrew Mak case study (first-party, verified via Google review with consent)
Itemised starter-kit breakdown (no competitor publishes this)
Free boarding after deposit — lodging AND food covered until the customer is ready — a MAXIMUM-gain differentiator with zero equivalent anywhere in the crawled competitor set
5-day nurse-back policy if the puppy falls sick — resolves the single biggest first-time-owner fear with a real, specific policy rather than a vague guarantee
Post-reservation process walkthrough (verified gap across all 3 crawled competitors)
AVS licence + smuggling-case pairing with real ICA/CNA figures (verified gap)
Lifestyle-first decision matrix instead of a static breed list
Licence/paperwork handled at purchase, stated plainly (no competitor states this either way)
